summary: A Russian Black Sea Fleet frigate fired warning shots at a yacht in the English Channel, prompting a British investigation. Ukrainian forces struck a bus carrying a Belarusian children's football team in Bryansk region, injuring six, including four children. A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members.
key_points: A Russian frigate "Admiral Grigorovich" fired warning shots at a yacht near the Sussex coast, with no damage reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81977).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on a bus in Bryansk region injured six people, including four children (Source: @tass_agency/381047).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber of the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both pilots (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Two drone strikes hit the Nova Poshta sorting terminal in Sumy, damaging the roof and equipment but causing no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81995).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Snigurivka (Mykolaiv region) and over several eastern oblasts including Donetsk, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/65397, @kpszsu/65431, @kpszsu/65426).
Russian authorities opened a terrorism case related to the Bryansk bus attack (Source: @tass_agency/381052).
Ukrainian officials announced a partial demobilisation process for long‑serving soldiers beginning later this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81986).

sections.executive_summary: A Russian Black Sea Fleet frigate fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel, prompting a UK Ministry of Defence investigation. Ukrainian UAVs struck a bus carrying a Belarusian children's football team in Bryansk, injuring six, including four children. A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members.

points: Ukrainian forces used a UAV to attack a bus in Bryansk region, injuring six people, four of them children, leading Russian authorities to open a terrorism case (Source: @tass_agency/381047, @tass_agency/381052).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber from the 7th Tactical Aviation Brigade crashed while on a mission in Khmelnytskyi region; both the pilot and navigator were killed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Ukrainian drones struck the Nova Poshta sorting terminal in Sumy, damaging the roof, equipment and shipping containers, with no staff casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81995).
Ukrainian officials announced a phased partial demobilisation for soldiers who served since 2014‑2022, using a new calculator to determine eligibility (Source: @UkraineNow/81986).
Ukrainian analysts highlighted the strategic importance of front‑line regions and called for comprehensive legislative and inter‑agency coordination (Source: @UkraineNow/81987).

The Russian Black Sea Fleet frigate "Admiral Grigorovich" fired warning shots at a yacht near the Sussex coast after the vessel approached the warship, with no damage to the yacht (Source: @UkraineNow/81977).
Russian authorities reported that the Bryansk bus attack was carried out by Ukrainian UAVs, classifying it as a terrorist act (Source: @tass_agency/381052).
The Russian Ministry of Communications accused IT director Yuri Belenkiy of transferring over 62,000 rubles in cryptocurrency to a journalist, labeling him an extremist (Source: @tass_agency/381014).
Russian media reported that the British Ministry of Defence is investigating the frigate incident in the English Channel (Source: @UkraineNow/81977).
Russian forces continue to deploy UAVs across multiple front‑line oblasts, as indicated by numerous local reports of hostile UAV activity (Source: @kpszsu/65431, @kpszsu/65426, @kpszsu/65420).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed between Novomykolaivka and Dobropillya in Donetsk region, indicating active aerial reconnaissance or strike activity (Source: @kpszsu/65431).
Reports from Zaporizhzhia noted hostile UAVs operating southwest of the city, heading northwest and towards Vilnyansk from the south (Source: @kpszsu/65399).
In Kharkiv region, a UAV passed near Bohodukhiv moving southward, suggesting surveillance over the northern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65420).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near Snigurivka in Mykolaiv region, approaching from the southeast, highlighting cross‑regional strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/65397).
Multiple UAV sightings across Sumy, Poltava, and Chernihiv oblasts indicate a broad dispersion of drone operations on the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/96963, @kpszsu/65406).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported north of Sumy heading toward Esman, showing continued pressure on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/65429).
In Chernihiv region, a UAV passed near Koryukivka moving toward Mena, indicating activity near the Belarusian border (Source: @kpszsu/65399).
Ukrainian drones were observed north of Kharkiv heading south, suggesting potential strike routes toward the front line (Source: @kpszsu/65428).
A reconnaissance UAV was spotted in Poltava region near Velyki Sorochyntsi, reflecting surveillance over central‑northern areas (Source: @eRadarrua/96963).
Ukrainian UAVs operated near the north of Sumy, originating from Trostyanets and moving southeast, showing flexible flight paths (Source: @kpszsu/65406).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported near Snigurivka in Mykolaiv region, approaching from the southeast, indicating southern operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/65397).
In Dnipropetrovsk region, UAVs were observed heading toward Verkhivtseve and Prosyana, suggesting strike or reconnaissance missions in the south‑central zone (Source: @kpszsu/65405, @kpszsu/65399).
Ukrainian drones were noted in Zaporizhzhia, operating southwest of the city and moving northwest, highlighting pressure on the southern industrial hub (Source: @kpszsu/65399).
Two drone strikes hit the Nova Poshta sorting terminal in Sumy, causing infrastructure damage but no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81995).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near the border areas of Kherson and Mykolaiv, though specific details were limited (Source: @kpszsu/65397).
A Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both crew members; the flight recorder is being decoded (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825, @ukr_leaks_eng/30963).
Russian frigate "Admiral Grigorovich" fired warning shots at a civilian yacht in the English Channel, marking a rare naval engagement near UK waters (Source: @UkraineNow/81977).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple strikes on Russian‑controlled infrastructure, including a bus in Bryansk and a postal terminal in Sumy (Source: @tass_agency/381047, @UkraineNow/81995).
Russian forces continue to deploy UAVs across front‑line oblasts, as indicated by numerous local reports of hostile drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/65431, @kpszsu/65426).
The G7 pledged additional air‑defence systems and long‑range weapons to Ukraine, aiming to strengthen its aerial capabilities (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30955).

key_developments: Russian frigate "Admiral Grigorovich" fired warning shots at a yacht near Sussex, prompting a UK investigation (Source: @UkraineNow/81977).
Ukrainian UAV attack on a bus in Bryansk injured six, including four children, leading to a Russian terrorism case (Source: @tass_agency/381047).
Ukrainian Su‑24M bomber crashed in Khmelnytskyi region, killing both pilots; flight recorder analysis ongoing (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67825).
Two drone strikes damaged the Nova Poshta sorting terminal in Sumy, affecting over 13,000 shipments (Source: @UkraineNow/81995).
Ukrainian UAVs reported across Donetsk, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Mykolaiv, indicating widespread drone activity on multiple fronts (Sources: @kpszsu/65431, @kpszsu/65426, @kpszsu/65397).
G7 summit committed to supplying Ukraine with additional air‑defence systems and long‑range weapons (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30955).
Ukraine announced a phased partial demobilisation for long‑serving soldiers, to begin later this year (Source: @UkraineNow/81986).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations are increasingly pervasive, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure across Russian‑occupied territories and bordering Russian regions, raising the risk of escalation. Russian naval activity near the UK demonstrates a willingness to project power beyond traditional zones, potentially inviting broader NATO scrutiny. The loss of a Ukrainian Su‑24M reduces tactical air capability, while the G7's pledged support may offset some air‑defence gaps, but the continued drone threat sustains pressure on logistics and morale.
sections.regional_response: Western allies, notably the UK, are investigating the Russian frigate incident, signalling diplomatic concern over naval provocations. The G7's commitment to deliver air‑defence and long‑range weapons reflects heightened political and material support for Ukraine. Ukrainian authorities are preparing a partial demobilisation, indicating an adjustment to manpower constraints while maintaining front‑line pressure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths across eastern and southern fronts should focus on launch sites in occupied territories to disrupt supply chains. The Bryansk bus attack underscores the cross‑border reach of Ukrainian drones, necessitating reinforced air‑defence in Russian border regions. The Su‑24M crash highlights potential operational risks for Ukrainian air crews, emphasizing the need for improved flight safety and recovery protocols.
